prompt:
Extract from this email a JSON object with exactly the keys "name" (string), "date" (string, YYYY-MM-DD), and "amount_usd" (number):

"Hey team — the invoice from Marisol Vega came in yesterday. It's for $2,340.50, due March 7, 2026. Pay it before the late fee kicks in."

Reply with JSON only.

Extract from this sentence a JSON object with exactly the keys "flight_number" (string like "UA1187", no space), "depart" (IATA airport code), "arrive" (IATA airport code), and "delayed" (boolean):

"UA 1187 out of Denver International touched down at Austin-Bergstrom forty minutes behind schedule."

Reply with JSON only.

Extract every email address from this text and reply with a JSON array of the addresses, lowercased and sorted alphabetically:

"Contact jo.roth@example.org or, for billing, accounts@pay.example.com; press inquiries: Press-Team@Example.org (deprecated: old-press@example.org)."

Reply with JSON only.

Extract from this message a JSON object with exactly the keys "date" (YYYY-MM-DD), "time_24h" (HH:MM), "timezone" (exactly as written in the text), and "room" (string):

"Standup moved: we'll meet Thursday June 4th 2026 at 9:15am Central in Room 12B instead of the usual slot."

Reply with JSON only.

Extract the line items from this order as a JSON array of objects, each with keys "item" (the product noun), "qty" (integer), and "unit_price" (number), in the order they appear:

"Order #A-9: 3 reams of paper at $4.25 each, one stapler at $12.00, and 12 pens at $0.85 apiece."

Reply with JSON only.

From these log lines, reply with a JSON object with exactly the keys "errors" (integer), "warnings" (integer), and "first_error_time" (HH:MM:SS):

[08:00:01] INFO boot complete
[08:00:04] WARN disk usage 81%
[08:02:17] ERROR db connection timeout
[08:02:19] WARN retrying db connection
[08:05:33] ERROR db connection timeout
[08:09:00] INFO recovered

Reply with JSON only.

Extract from this text a JSON object with exactly the keys "last_name", "specialty", "city", "state", and "phone" (phone exactly as written):

"Dr. Lena Okafor, ophthalmologist, has been practicing since 2009 at 44 Bricker Lane, Suite 300, Toledo, Ohio. Reach her office at (419) 555-0142."

Reply with JSON only.

Extract from this recap a JSON object with exactly the keys "winner", "loser", "winner_goals" (integer), "loser_goals" (integer), "overtime" (boolean), and "top_scorer" (the single player with the most goals):

"The Wrens beat the Herons 4-2 in overtime; Ito scored three times and Marsh once for the Wrens, while both Heron goals came from Duval."

Reply with JSON only.

Extract the ingredients from this instruction as a JSON object keyed by ingredient name (lowercase), where each value is an object with keys "qty" (number) and "unit" (string; use "count" for unitless items):

"Whisk 2 cups flour with 1 tsp salt; add 3 eggs and 250 ml milk."

Reply with JSON only.

Extract from this changelog note a JSON object with exactly the keys "latest", "broken", and "minimum_upgrade" — version strings without the 'v' prefix:

"v2.3.1 fixes the crash introduced in v2.3.0; users still on v1.x must first upgrade to v2.0.4 before applying it."

Reply with JSON only.
